question: Model a `Course` class that aggregates `Student` objects. The `Course` class should have a `courseName` (string) and maintain a collection (e.g., `std::vector`) of `Student` objects. Implement methods to add a student to the course and display all enrolled students.

test_cases.expected:
--- Testing Readability/Efficiency Issues ---
Student Eve Adams (ID: 201) added to Intro to C++.
Student Frank Green (ID: 202) added to Intro to C++.
Student Grace Hall (ID: 203) added to Intro to C++.

--- Course Details for: Intro to C++ ---
Current Enrollment (3 students):
  - ID: 201, Name: Eve Adams
  - ID: 202, Name: Frank Green
  - ID: 203, Name: Grace Hall
----------------------------------------

--- Course Details for: Data Structures ---
  No students currently enrolled.
----------------------------------------

errors: The `addStudent` method passes `Student` objects by value, causing unnecessary object copying when a `const` reference would be more efficient.
The `displayStudents` method uses an indexed `for` loop instead of a more modern and readable range-based `for` loop for iterating through the collection.
Inconsistent naming conventions are used (e.g., `course_name` vs `displayStudents`, `display_info` vs `displayStudents`), reducing readability.
Less descriptive parameter names are used in constructors and methods (e.g., `n`, `i`, `c_name`, `s`), which can hinder understanding.
